title: 8.-Contract: Alameda-Contra Costa County Transit District (AC Transit) for EasyPass Program 2026-2028

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution authorizing the City Manager to execute an agreement and any
amendments with the Alameda-Contra Costa County Transit District (AC Transit) for the
continuation of an annual bus transit pass (EasyPass) program for City of Berkeley
employees in an amount not to exceed $572,717 for a three-year period covering 2026,
2027 and 2028.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
The total cost for three years is $572,717 based on the current eligible employee pool of
1,402 employees. AC Transit invoices organizations based on the total number of
eligible employees. Funds for the EasyPass program are found in Payroll Deduction
Trust Fund 013-99-900-900-0000-000-000-201599. The Payroll Deduction Trust Fund is
funded by multiple funds based on employee labor distributions which are influenced by
payroll deductions. The City will process payment on an annual basis.

sections.current_situation:
The current transit pass contract with AC Transit expires on December 31, 2025. Staff is
recommending that Council authorize a new contract to continue the program through
December 31, 2028 for a three-year period.
The City and AC Transit formed a partnership to establish an employee transit pass
program in 2002 to provide all eligible City employees unlimited rides on all AC Transit
buses. Whereas the Public Works department established and managed the AC Transit
contract, it is now being managed by the Human Resources department as it is a
benefit provided to City Staff. About 800 employees are currently enrolled.
By continuing to provide this employee benefit program, the City remains a model
employer for Berkeley businesses required to offer a Commuter Benefit Program
(Berkeley Municipal Code 9.88) or Trip Reduction Information Program.
Maintaining funding for AC Transit’s Easy Pass for City staff is in alignment with the
City’s Strategic Plan Priorities, advancing the goal to be a global leader in addressing
climate change, advancing environmental justice, and protecting the environment, while
attracting and retaining a talented and diverse City government workforce.
sections.background:
Council previously approved extension of the program since its inception in 2002, most
recently in 2021 (Resolution 69,663-N.S.) and in 2015 (Resolution No. 66,888-N.S.)
while in 2025 additional funds were added to pay the remaining invoice for 2025
(Resolution 71,938-N.S.). While it is customary for the City to extend Requests for
Proposals to manage expenditures of this size and duration, AC Transit is the sole
public transit provider able to meet the program requirement and the City must execute
a contract directly with the public agency.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
By maintaining the AC Transit EasyPass Program, the City is reducing vehicle miles
traveled in individual vehicles and reducing associated greenhouse gasses by
encouraging staff to utilize public transit.
sections.rationale:
The EasyPass program is an employee benefit that staff rely on to get to and from work
since 2002. As part of the commuter benefits program, the AC Transit EasyPass
provides eligible employees with unlimited access to public transit. This initiative
supports the City’s strategic goals related to sustainability, equity, and workforce well-
being by encouraging public transportation use, reducing traffic and emissions, and
improving access to affordable commuting options. The program also strengthens
recruitment and retention efforts and may offer tax efficiencies under IRS commuter
benefit guidelines.

October 28, 2025
sections.alternatives:
There is not another public transit agency that can provide the associated services
within this contract. This is an ongoing benefit provided to City staff, which staff rely on
to commute to their associated City workplaces.
CONTACT PERSON
Janelle Rodrigues, Director of Human Resources, Human Resources, (510) 981-6807

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
ALAMEDA-CONTRA COSTA COUNTY TRANSIT DISTRICT (AC TRANSIT) FOR
EASYPASS PROGRAM 2026-2028
WHEREAS, in January 2002 the City and AC Transit formed a partnership to establish
an employee transit pass program to provide all eligible City employees unlimited rides
on all AC Transit buses; and
WHEREAS, the concentration of public bus service in Berkeley and the size of the City's
workforce support an employee pass program with the Alameda-Contra Costa Transit
District (AC Transit); and
WHEREAS, the EasyPass program is a proven and popular benefit encouraging use of
public transit for City employees, and contributing to reduction in the use of single
occupancy vehicles for work and recreational travel; and
WHEREAS, by continuing to provide this employee benefit program, the City remains a
model employer for Berkeley businesses required to offer a Commuter Benefit Program
(Berkeley Municipal Code 9.88) or Trip Reduction Information Program; and
WHEREAS, since forming a partnership in 2002 to establish the EasyPass (then
EcoPass) Program, the City of Berkeley and AC Transit have offered employees unlimited
usage passes for all AC Transit local and Transbay buses; and
WHEREAS, the Council has approved extension of the program since its inception in
2002, again in 2015 (Resolution No. 66,888-N.S.), in 2021 (Resolution 69,663-N.S.), and
most recently has authorized adding funds to pay for the remaining costs of the program
in 2025 (Resolution 71,938-N.S); and
WHEREAS, the current contract expires December 31, 2025, and a new contract is
needed for the 2026-2028 period in a not to exceed amount of $572,717.00.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the
City Manager, or designee, is authorized to execute a contract with the Alameda-
Contra Costa County Transit District (AC Transit) for the continuation of the EasyPass
annual bus transit pass program for City of Berkeley employees for th
